metadata.title: States PCS: Andhra Pradesh GK For APPSC Examinations

questions.question: Which Scheduled Tribe, also known as Kampu or Kapu, is primarily found in the Visakhapatnam and Vizianagaram districts of Andhra Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Bagata tribe, a Scheduled Tribe in Andhra Pradesh, is mainly found in Visakhapatnam, Vizianagaram, East Godavari, and Rangareddy districts. They are Telugu-speaking fishermen, known locally as Kampu/Kapu, with Proto-Australoid traits and distinct socio-cultural characteristics.

questions.question: Who played a key role in introducing and spreading the principles of Brahmo Samaj in Andhra Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Raghupati Venkataratnam Naidu founded the Brahmo Mandir at Narsapur in 1884, championing social reforms and Brahmo Samaj ideals in Andhra Pradesh.

questions.question: In which language and script are most Satavahana dynasty inscriptions found?
questions.explanation: Satavahana inscriptions are primarily in Maharashtri Prakrit language written using the Brahmi script, especially in Andhra Pradesh.

questions.question: What was the name given to the trade associations that promoted commerce during the Satavahana period in Andhra Pradesh?
questions.explanation: During the Satavahana period, trade and artisan associations were called Guilds or Srenulu; they regulated trade, established standards, managed trade routes, and made endowments, as seen in inscriptions at Amaravati, Nagarjunakonda, and Kondapur.

questions.question: Who succeeded Betharaju (Beta I) as the ruler among the Kakatiyas and is known for expanding their territories around Warangal in Andhra Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Prola Raju (Prola I), son of Betharaju (Beta I), expanded Kakatiya rule in the 12th century, setting foundations for prominent successors in Andhra Pradesh.

questions.question: Whom did Kakatiya ruler Rudra Deva assist during the Battle of Palanadu in the 12th century?
questions.explanation: Kakatiya Rudra Deva provided military support to Nalagama Raju in the Battle of Palanadu, aiding his victory over Malideva Raju and reinforcing Kakatiya hold on the region.

questions.question: Who is referred to as “Vinaya Bhushan” in the Draksharama temple inscription of 1187 CE?
questions.explanation: The 1187 CE Draksharama inscription describes the Kakatiya ruler Rudra Deva as “Vinaya Bhushan,” highlighting his humility and support for Shaiva-Shakta traditions in the Godavari delta.

questions.question: Who served as the editor of the Telugu newspaper Golconda Patrika during the 1950s, known for supporting regional language rights and reporting on Andhra statehood movements?
questions.explanation: Suvaram Pratap Reddy was the editor of Golconda Patrika, a prominent Telugu daily from Hyderabad established in 1924, and played a key role in reporting on Andhra issues.

questions.question: From which city is the Telugu monthly magazine ‘Nadustunna Charitra’ published?
questions.explanation: ‘Nadustunna Charitra’ is published monthly from Vijayawada by the Andhra Pradesh Historical Research Society since 2015.

questions.question: In which Indian state did the Naxalite movement first originate in 1967?
questions.explanation: The Naxalite movement started in 1967 at Naxalbari, West Bengal, led by CPI (Marxist) leaders Kanu Sanyal and Charu Majumdar, influenced by Maoist ideology.
